title: Optimal use of Nuclear Power plant

content.problemStatement:
It is now 2050 AD. A city will be built under large protective circular dome. A nuclear power plant **(NPP)**  built outside the city will power the whole city. For safety reasons the **NPP** must be at least **dmin **distance away from the dome. The radius of the dome has not been fixed yet. The houses inside the dome will be built after building the dome. So for simplicity it can be assumed that each location under the dome is equally likely to have a house. In other words houses will be uniformly distributed in the land inside the dome. As the houses are full of modern electrical equipments, so the power consumption per house is quite high. So each house is connected with the **NPP** with the shortest possible cable underground. In the figure below you can see four houses (Blue circles) and their shortest connection with **NPP** (dotted line). For wiring cost and line-loss issues the average distance of the houses (assuming they are uniformly distributed under then dome) from the **NPP** cannot be more than **Davg**. 


Given the value of **Davg, dmin** your job is to find the maximum possible value of the radius of the land under dome.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two strictly positive integers Davg and dmin, separated by a space.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a floating-point number Rmax, which denotes the maximum possible value of the radius of the circular land under the dome, rounded to four digits after the decimal point.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, the maximum possible radius of the circular land under the dome is approximately 72.6679. For the second test case, the maximum possible radius is approximately 4.7114.

solution.approach: To find the maximum possible radius of the circular land under the dome, we need to consider the average distance of the houses from the NPP and the minimum distance between the NPP and the dome. We can use the formula for the average distance of points in a circle from a point outside the circle, which is (2/3) * (R + dmin) * (R / (R + dmin)) + (1/3) * dmin, where R is the radius of the circle. We can set this expression equal to Davg and solve for R.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) — each test case requires a constant amount of work to solve the equation for R
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — we only need a constant amount of space to store the input and output values

code.Python3:
import math
T = int(input())
for _ in range(T):
    Davg, dmin = map(float, input().split())
    R = (3 * Davg - dmin) / 2
    print(f"{R:.4f}")
